nodes.label: AI for summarization and research
nodes.grounding_span: summarize longer emails sent to me by team members, so I can make sure that I am understanding the project details correctly

nodes.label: AI as writing assistant for drafting
nodes.grounding_span: help me draft the codebases for some software projects

nodes.label: AI collaboration based on implementation specificity
nodes.grounding_span: If I have specific plan on how I want a feature implemented, I'll do the tweak myself, but if I'm less specific with an implementation, and want to try different versions, I'll go back and worth with the AI

nodes.label: AI as coding assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI use for non-coding tasks like scheduling and emails
nodes.grounding_span: For anything related to writing code, I am always willing to work with AI. For other tasks, like scheduling, and writing emails I usually do them myself

nodes.label: AI for repetitive simple tasks
nodes.grounding_span: These tasks feel simple enough that I'm not sure if using AI will actually save me much time

nodes.label: AI as knowledge resource for unfamiliar domains
nodes.label_negative: AI as unnecessary for familiar domains
nodes.grounding_span: I was unfamiliar with that specific type of database, so the AI was really helpful for the task

nodes.label: AI impact on skill maintenance
nodes.label_negative: Personal skill maintenance despite AI improvement
nodes.grounding_span: I don't spend too much extra time with maintaining skills in areas where AI could take over. AI will probably continue to get better at coding at a rate faster than me
